answer:
Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source support three primary authentication methods depending on your environment and use case. For standard installations, third-party applications use OAuth 1.0a [1][2], mobile and automated applications use token-based authentication [1][3], and administrators or customers authenticate via login credentials to obtain access tokens [1][2].

In Adobe Commerce as a Cloud Service, all REST API requests must be authenticated using Adobe Identity Management Service (IMS) via OAuth 2.0 protocols [1][4]. This requires signing every request with an IMS Access Token passed in the Authorization header [1][4]:

```http
Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
```

For non-cloud setups, integrations can also use OAuth 1.0a or bearer tokens if explicitly enabled in the admin configuration [5][6][3].

results.title: Getting Started with Adobe Commerce Web APIs
Authentication in Adobe Commerce as a Cloud Service
